name: Solionye Ogurtsy- Brined Cucumbers
recipeIngredient: 1 Dill plant, bunch mature, seeds included
1/2 oz Hot red pepper, fresh seeded
recipeInstructions:
Preparing Cucumbers: Wash the cucumbers under running water and place in a bowl. Cover with ice water and enough ice to keep them cold for 10 hours. Drain the cucumbers and trim both ends so that the brine will more easily penetrate the flesh.
Assembling: Rinse the dill and divide into two halves. Scrub and rinse the horseradish and slice thinly. Peel and halve the garlic. Fold one half of the dill into a ring to fit the bottom of the pickling jar. Scatter horseradish, garlic, red pepper, and 1/3 of the tarragon on top. Arrange half of the cucumbers upright in the jar, packing them tightly. Place the second third of the tarragon on top, then pack in the rest of the cucumbers in the same manner. Layer the remaining dill, horseradish, garlic, pepper, and tarragon over them.
Combine the salt and water, stirring until the salt dissolves. The percentage of salt in this brine is 5.5 to 6.5 percent. Pour the brine into the jar. Ensure that the cucumbers and herbs are fully submerged. Cover with a saucer and weight down with a jar or can filled with water. Cover the mouth of the jar with 2 layers of cheesecloth and tie in place with string.
Fermentation and storage: For the first 2-3 days, keep the cucumbers on a well-aired shaded patio or similar spot with a temperature of 64-68 degrees. Remove scum as it appears and wash the mouth of the jar daily. After 3-4 days when the most active fermentation has subsided slightly, remove the weight and lid. Check that the brine still covers the cucumbers. If not, add brine in the same proportions (1 1/4-1 3/4 tbsp salt to 1 pint water). Cover the jar with its own lid and refrigerate at 32-38 degrees. During the next 10-14 days, ensure that the brine does not become too cloudy (there is always some degree of cloudiness due to the fermentation process) and that there is no mold. If either occurs, replace the brine, rinse the upper layer of dill, wipe the mouth of the jar, and close again. Store the pickles on the lowest shelf of the refrigerator. They will keep for up to 1 week.
